component.md defines ONLY the 8-param Component.Of(family, designation, section, coring, standard, capacityKey, appearanceId, key) at line 346, but all four Minor family pages (joint.md, fastener.md:315, connector.md:352, reinforcement.md:388) call the 6-param form Component.Of(family, designation, section, capacityKey, appearanceId, key) — these 6-param calls do not bind to the single 8-param definition. ComponentAuthority (component.md:108-117) has no AWS entry, so a weld cannot cite a ComponentStandard, confirming the Minor families are meant to use a convenience overload. Resolution: component.md adds a 6-param Component.Of overload defaulting coring=Coring.None and a neutral ComponentStandard for the Minor families (the polymorphic-entry pattern), OR the four Minor family pages thread Coring.None + a standard into the 8-param call. joint.md already matches the sibling 6-param convention; the owner overload is the cross-file fix.

fastener.md:321, connector.md:371, and reinforcement.md:394 thread ComparerAccessors.StringOrdinal.EqualityComparer (an IEqualityComparer<string>) into BuildXRows .ToFrozenDictionary on a ComponentId key — a CS1503 type mismatch (the parameter is IEqualityComparer<ComponentId>, invariant in T). component.md's ComponentCatalogue.Build (line 525) and the rebuilt joint.md BuildJointRows both correctly omit it because ComponentId's generated [KeyMemberEqualityComparer<ComparerAccessors.StringOrdinal, string>] ordinal value-equality keys the frozen dictionary via EqualityComparer<ComponentId>.Default. Remove the comparer argument from the three siblings' .ToFrozenDictionary calls.

The [1].5 ONE neutral detail schema is not yet a declared owner: property.md owns PropertyValue/PropertyName/PropertyBag(ValueBag<V>)/InheritanceMode but declares no named neutral detail schema, and the realizing-detail bag survives only as the IFC-named Rasm_ConnectionRealization bag in the Materials Connection/Component pages. property.md (the [1].5 owner) must formalize the ONE neutral detail schema — the canonical PropertyName vocabulary over PropertyBag — that BOTH the realizing-detail bag (the former Rasm_ConnectionRealization, carrying neutral PropertyName rows JointType/FastenerType/AccessoryType/BarType/NominalDiameter with no IFC Pset name on the seam) AND the material-property->Pset lowering target; material.md now references property#PROPERTY_BAG for the material-property egress, so the owner-side declaration must back it.

The named coherence flag (quantity.md<->material.md): quantity.md sanctions QuantityType.Create domain names but its lead paragraph frames the discriminator as 'the UnitsNet QuantityInfo.Name' and its [02]-[DIMENSION] body explicitly names only SectionModulus/SecondMomentOfArea (not TorsionConstant/WarpingConstant). quantity.md must EXPLICITLY enumerate all four engineering-section QuantityType domain tokens the SeamSection chain mints (SectionModulus, SecondMomentOfArea, TorsionConstant, WarpingConstant) in its open-Create sanction AND reconcile the lead 'the discriminator IS the UnitsNet QuantityInfo.Name' framing so it does not read as excluding consumer-minted domain names, so the Materials Projection/component.md SeamSection (MeasureValue.OfSi(QuantityType.Create(...), Dimension.Create(4/6,...), _)) cites no discriminator quantity.md has not blessed by name. Dimension.Create(int x7) is confirmed real (the [ComplexValueObject] generated factory used in every quantity.md static dimension row); no phantom.

Field-count mismatch the projection's SeamSection bridges by zeroing: the Component ComputedSection is 17-field and lacks ShearCentreY/ShearCentreZ/MonosymmetryFactor, while the seam SectionProperties (Composition/material.md) is now 20-field and carries them for the EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.2 general LTB route. SeamSection stamps the three asymmetry columns ZERO (exact for every doubly-symmetric/parametric family, which the seam IsDoublySymmetric reads as symmetric), so an asymmetric open thin-walled steel section (channel/tee/angle) cannot flexural-torsional-buckling-check off the seam until component.md's ComputedSection grows the three columns and steel.md's SteelStiffness computes the shear-centre offsets + monosymmetry factor; the projection's SeamSection then lifts them faithfully. The page's [SECTION_BAKE_TWENTY_FIELD] research note documents this as the one cross-file widening.

Add the [1].1 deterministic Type-Object NodeId derivation the ARCHITECTURE.md endpoint now declares (the line-41 ComponentProjector row + the [CONTENT_KEY_IDIOM] one-regime-two-seedings bullet). As of last read the NodeId owner mints only Rooted() (Guid-v7), Content(bytes), and OfContent(addr), and Node.Object's ToCanonicalBytes arm writes Representations unconditionally — there is NO Type-seed projection that hashes the Object canonical bytes EXCLUDING the volatile Representations so a Type's rooted id derives deterministically from the Component content (and a later geometry attach never re-keys the Type, identical Components dedup to ONE Type, IfcElementType round-trip stable). element.md is concurrently mid-rebuild and has already landed [1].4/[1].6 (BakedMaterial line 419, TypeBinding line 428, Element.Type/TypeId 441/446, the BakeObject named Seq-field inheritance via TypeResolutionOf/UnionBy 602-617); verify/add only the Type-seed mint (e.g. a NodeId derivation over a Representations-excluded ToCanonicalBytes overload the Materials ComponentProjector composes) to close [1].1.

Land the deterministic Type-seed NodeId derivation projection.md's owner-mints law composes (the coupled prerequisite — the path it composes must land here). In [02]-[NODE_MODEL] add a rooted NodeId mint that is a pure function of an Object's canonical Type-seed bytes (rooted, NOT Guid-v7, NOT a content-hash in the non-rooted namespace), and split Object.ToCanonicalBytes (line 292) so the Type seed EXCLUDES the volatile Representations map (the ONLY sanctioned change to the Object arm per [1].1) — so identical Components dedup to one Type Object and a later Body/geometry attach never re-keys it. Without this member the ComponentProjector's deterministic Type mint has no owner. Also land the remaining [1].4/[1].6 element.md unified-paradigm legs the seam assumes: MaterialBinding (element.md:379)→BakedMaterial, the new TypeBinding (NodeId TypeId + inherited type-level BakedMaterial set/SectionProperties/Classifications), the TypeId reference on the baked Element, and the named Bake type→occurrence inheritance with explicit per-field precedence (single fields = occurrence-overrides-type, Seq fields = union + dedup-by-key).

Resolve the [1].6 MaterialBinding trio: element.md:379 MaterialBinding→BakedMaterial (the Bake-folded element→material binding) and add TypeBinding (the Bake-produced NodeId TypeId + inherited type-level BakedMaterial set/Section/Classifications), with the [1].4 one named Bake type→occurrence inheritance; the Materials projection MaterialBinding name is reserved (no rename). Triggered because the Component owner reuses ObjectKind.Type and the COMPONENT_PROJECTOR mints the deterministic-rooted Type Object the Bake inherits from.

The owner-mints-its-Type-identity model requires three new/changed seam members the ComponentProjector composes but element.md does not yet declare: (1) NodeId.Type(ReadOnlySpan<byte> typeSeed) — a deterministic-rooted Type-id derivation (a rooted identity derived from the type-seed content so identical Components dedup to one Type and IFC round-trip is stable, distinct from NodeId.Rooted()'s unique Guid-v7 and NodeId.Content()'s non-rooted content hash); (2) Node.Object.ToTypeSeedBytes(double tolerance) — the canonical projection EXCLUDING the volatile Representations (so a later geometry attach never re-keys the Type), distinct from the full ToCanonicalBytes the Occurrence content key uses; (3) the MaterialBinding record at element.md:379 renamed to BakedMaterial (the Bake-folded element->material binding) to free the MaterialBinding name for the Materials projection, with the Element.Materials field, the BakeObject Associate-Material arm, and the [03] prose updated. Also add TypeBinding (Bake-produced: NodeId TypeId + the inherited type-level BakedMaterial set/Section/Classifications) surfaced on the baked Element, and rework the Bake fold + SectionOf/CompositionOf/MaterialsOf to resolve material/section/classification THROUGH the Assign.TypeDefinition edge (the type->occurrence inheritance) WITHOUT breaking the Op-free graph.SectionOf(member).

The seam CONTRACT for the Type/Occurrence collapse is landed at both ARCHITECTURE endpoints and ObjectKind.Type/AssignKind.TypeDefinition/DetailSchema are realized, but the element.md code FENCES for the new identity/inheritance vocabulary appear unrealized: the deterministic Type-seed NodeId derivation (Object.ToCanonicalBytes excluding the volatile Representations for the Type seed, [1].1), the TypeBinding (NodeId TypeId) record and the BakedMaterial rename of the element->material binding ([1].6), and the type-resolved rework of occurrence SectionOf/CompositionOf/MaterialsOf that must NOT break Compute's Op-free graph.SectionOf(member) ([1].4). This is WF-1 Element-folder body realization (could not be confirmed present in element.md).

element.md's `Bake` fold has NOT landed the [1].4/[1].6 paradigm that relation.md now asserts. It still gathers only the type's property/quantity bags for the narrow PropertyBag-only `InheritanceMode` merge (lines 354, 540 — 'gathers its bags for the InheritanceMode merge'), still folds the material into `MaterialBinding` (record at line 379; constructed lines 519/524), and declares no `TypeBinding`/`TypeId`. To match relation.md's `Assign.TypeDefinition` contract, element.md must: (1) broaden the type→occurrence `Bake` merge to the NAMED inheritance over all standardized fields — single fields occurrence-overrides-type, `Seq` fields (materials/assessments/classifications) union + dedup-by-key — with `InheritanceMode.Resolve` retained only as the inner value-bag precedence; (2) rename `MaterialBinding`→`BakedMaterial` (the [1].6 trio: `BakedMaterial` Element-baked / `MaterialBinding` Materials-projection / `TypeBinding` new); (3) add `TypeBinding` (NodeId `TypeId` + inherited type-level `BakedMaterial` set/Section/Classifications); (4) surface a `TypeId` reference on the baked `Element` so the generator recovers the inherited Component.

The deterministic Type id (NodeId.RootedType over the Representations-excluded Node.Object.ToTypeSeedBytes) newly admits a Verify dual that address.md's CONTENT_ADDRESS Verify lacks: Verify recomputes the content-self-hash for NON-rooted nodes and skips rooted nodes, but a TYPE Object's rooted id is now deterministic (unlike an Occurrence's random Guid), so a rooted-Type Verify arm — recompute NodeId.RootedType(((Node.Object)n).ToTypeSeedBytes(header.Tolerance).Span) and compare to the stored id — is the natural integrity dual catching a forged/corrupted Type id. element.md supplies the mechanism (id-excluded ToTypeSeedBytes + RootedType); address.md owns Verify. Optional integrity hardening, not a correctness break (rooted ids were never Verify-checked).
